for n in before:
    if n['color'] in 'NbR' and n['boundary'][0][0] < 50:
        b = n['boundary']
        min_c = min(p[1] for p in b)
        max_c = max(p[1] for p in b)
        print(f"  {n['color']}: cols={min_c}-{max_c}")

print("\n=== After ACTION7 ===")
for n in after:
    if n['color'] in 'NbR' and n['boundary'][0][0] < 50:
        b = n['boundary']
        min_c = min(p[1] for p in b)
        max_c = max(p[1] for p in b)
        print(f"  {n['color']}: cols={min_c}-{max_c}")

# Check ascii
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\n=== Row 37-40 ===")
for i in range(36, 42):

</parameter>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The blocks didn't move. The 4 cells that changed must be something else. Let me compare the ascii more carefully. Looking at row 38:
Before: BBBMWMMWwcNNNNccbbbbccccccccccccccRRRRcBBB
After:  BBBMWMMWwgNNNNwcbbbbccccccccccccccRRRRcBBB
The difference is at col 16 (0-indexed from the crop start at col 8):
Before: ...WwcN...
After:  ...WwgN...
And at col 22:
Before: ...Ncc...
After:  ...Nwc...
So ACTION7 seems to have changed some cells in the path area. It looks like it extended the path by 1 cell to the right (from col 16 to col 17, and from col 21 to col 22).
Wait, let me look more carefully:
Row 38 before: BBBMWMMWwcNNNNccbbbb...
Row 38 after:  BBBMWMMWwgNNNNwcbbbb...
The 'c' at position 16 (relative to crop) changed to 'g', and the 'c' at position 22 changed to 'w'. This looks like the path extended by 1 segment.
So ACTION7 is equivalent to RIGHT - it extends the path to the right! Let me verify by checking if pressing RIGHT again extends further.
Actually wait, let me re-examine. Before ACTION7, the path was at cols 12-15 (W) with some w cells. After ACTION7, the path seems to have extended. Let me check the w cells more carefully.
Actually, looking at the row 38 comparison:
Before: ...WwcNN... (c at col 16, c at col 22)
After:  ...WwgNN... (g at col 16, w at col 22)
So col 16 changed from c to g, and col 22 changed from c to w. This is a 2-cell change on row 38, and similarly on row 39. That's 4 cells total, which matches the animation.
So ACTION7 extends the path by 1 segment (2 cells: one on row 38 and one on row 39). This is the same as RIGHT!
Let me try pressing RIGHT to see if it also extends by 1 segment.

action(['RIGHT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
